metadata.title: The Loneliness of Mr. Tan
metadata.description: An elderly patient struggling with loneliness in the hospital

start.text: Oh, hello nurse... I've been lying here for hours. Everyone seems so busy.

options.text: How are you feeling today, Mr. Tan?
options.response: Physically? The doctors say I'm improving. But...

options.text: I need to check your vitals quickly.
options.response: Of course... always in a hurry. I understand.

feeling_lonely.text: But emotionally? I feel invisible. My family hasn't visited this week.

options.text: That must be really difficult for you.
options.response: You know... you're the first person to actually ask how I'm doing. Not just about my blood pressure.
Yes... busy. Everyone is always busy...

rushed_interaction.text: You all have so many patients. I shouldn't complain. I'm just... another bed number.

options.text: Wait, you're not just a number to me.
I really do need to finish my rounds.
options.response: Yes, of course. I'll be here... alone.

opening_up.text: My wife passed three years ago. The kids have their own lives now. This hospital bed... it's just so quiet.

options.text: Would you like to tell me about your wife?
options.response: Would I? Oh, she was wonderful... let me tell you about her.

options.text: I can sit with you for a few minutes.
options.response: Just your presence... it means more than you know.

sharing_memories.text: She loved gardening. Every morning she'd be out there, talking to her flowers. She said they grew better when you spoke kindly to them. Maybe people do too.

options.response: She was. And you know what? You remind me of her kindness.

comfortable_silence.text: You know, sometimes the best medicine isn't in those pills. It's moments like this.

options.text: I'll try to check on you more often.
options.response: I'd like that very much. Thank you, truly.

connection_made.text: Before you go... what's your name? I'd like to remember the person who made me feel human again.

options.text: I'm happy I could be here for you today.
options.response: The best nurses aren't just skilled. They're kind. Like you.

redemption.text: I've been feeling so invisible. But you stopped. You saw me. That's more than most people have done.

options.text: Everyone deserves to be seen and heard.
options.response: Thank you. I won't forget this kindness.

dismissed.text: I should probably rest anyway. Thank you for your time, nurse.
